name: Canonical add-expense dialog and independent group/source refactor
description: Replaced quick-add and the dedicated `/expenses/new` create surface with one global `AddExpenseDialog`, added dialog-only VND thousand-shortcut amount entry, fixed category-picker dialog nesting, switched canonical source keys to `cash|bank-transfer|card|momo|zalo-pay|shopee-pay|other`, and decoupled expense groups from household scope so expenses may be private-only, household-only, group-only, or household+group.

evidence.description: New canonical create dialog owns source/category/household/group queries, VND amount helper usage, validation, create mutation, profile last-source persistence, and undo toast flow without sending payerUserId.
Global provider mounts one shared AddExpenseDialog, preserves FAB + q shortcut behavior, and exposes openDialog/closeDialog for all entry points.
Expenses page header CTA now opens the dialog, supports `/expenses?add-expense=1`, and merges personal plus household group options for the filter surface.
Combobox content now supports `portal={false}` so dialog-hosted category selection stays inside the dialog focus/overlay boundary.
Worker migration decouples group storage/assignment from household scope and remaps legacy `e-wallet` rows to `momo`.
Product spec now documents the canonical add-expense dialog, VND shortcut amount semantics, independent family/group decisions, and retirement of `/expenses/new`.
Focused web helper test verified `3 -> 3.000 đ` display and `3000` submit amount behavior.
Repository typecheck passed after removing stale route types and aligning nullable group household typing.
Full repository test target passed after updating stale worker contract/integrity tests to match the new source/group product truth.
Final full verification passed, including install, harness, lint, typecheck, test, and GitNexus sync steps.

Final GitNexus change detection found high risk with affected processes in onboarding, create-expense, and group list/assignment/read flows, so those paths deserve focused regression attention when reviewing the diff.
